title: Event routing with phx-value-* and handle_info debounce
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/sortable_table_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SortableTableLive). The template renders clickable column headers with phx-click="sort" phx-value-column={col} phx-value-direction={next_direction(col)}. handle_event("sort", %{"column" => column, "direction" => direction}, socket) pattern-matches BOTH phx-value-* keys and pushes a handle_info(:reload, _) message via send(self(), :reload). handle_info(:reload, socket) reloads from MyApp.Table.query(socket.assigns.column, socket.assigns.direction) — but ONLY if the socket is connected?/1 (silently no-op on disconnected). Use to_form for the search input. The reload must be debounced: if another :reload arrives within 300ms, the first is dropped via Process.cancel_timer/1 on a timer reference stored in socket assigns.
tier_rationale: Combines phx-value-* multi-key routing with a send-to-self + debounced handle_info pattern. The timer cancel/reschedule dance is subtle. Sonnet typically picks per-direction event names (phx-click=sort_asc) or never debounces.
